vulkanmod.options.advCulling.tooltip: Use a culling algorithm that might improve performance by reducing the number of non visible chunk sections rendered.
vulkanmod.options.ao.subBlock.tooltip: Enables smooth lighting for non full block (experimental).
vulkanmod.options.entityCulling.tooltip: Enables culling for entities on not visible sections.
vulkanmod.options.frameQueue.tooltip: Higher values might help stabilize frametime but will increase input lag.
vulkanmod.options.backfaceCulling.toolti: Culls not visible block faces on the CPU improving GPU performance. To prevent increased CPU overhead enable Indirect Draw.
vulkanmod.options.indirectDraw.tooltip: Reduces CPU overhead but might increase GPU overhead.
vulkanmod.options.uniqueOpaqueLayer.tool: Use a unique render layer for opaque terrain to improve performance.
vulkanmod.options.wayland.tooltip: Use Linux Wayland compositor if available (Experimental). Requires restart.
vulkanmod.options.rt.enabled.tooltip: Hardware path tracing: real shadows, reflections, refraction and colored light. Follows the Shaders toggle (a shaders-without-RT mode is in development).
vulkanmod.options.shaders.enabled.toolti: Master picture toggle: ON = full ray tracing with our post (AgX grade, effects), OFF = plain rasterized VulkanMod. A shaders-without-RT mode is in development.
vulkanmod.options.rt.shadows.tooltip: A shadow ray towards the sun for every pixel. Off = the world is lit with no direct shadows.
vulkanmod.options.rt.ambient.tooltip: Rays per pixel that check how much sky reaches this spot. Corners and undergrowth get darker. Off = sky lights everything evenly (cheap, flat).
vulkanmod.options.rt.reflections.tooltip: Traced reflections in water, glass and metals. Off = they mirror the sky only.
vulkanmod.options.rt.clouds.tooltip: Clouds marched along the ray, with shadows on the world. Off = clear sky.
vulkanmod.options.rt.godrays.tooltip: Sun and moon shafts in the air and underwater. One of the heaviest effects: a shadow ray is traced at every march step. Turn this off first if FPS is low.
vulkanmod.options.rt.lights.tooltip: Torches, lava, lanterns and glowing blocks cast their own colored light with real shadows.
vulkanmod.options.img.motionBlur.tooltip: Smears the image along the motion of each pixel, like a real shutter collecting light. Blurs fast movement, not the whole screen.
vulkanmod.options.img.exposure.tooltip: Shifts auto-exposure in stops: +1.0 EV is twice as bright.
vulkanmod.options.img.saturation.tooltip: Color intensity. 0% is black and white, 100% is as shot.
vulkanmod.options.img.vignette.tooltip: Darkens the corners of the frame, like a real lens does.
vulkanmod.options.img.grain.tooltip: Fine noise, strongest in the shadows — as on film.
vulkanmod.options.img.chromatic.tooltip: The lens splits colors towards the edges of the frame.
vulkanmod.options.img.flare.tooltip: Sun and moon glare inside the lens: a halo and ghost reflections through the center of the frame. Fades out when the light source is hidden behind geometry.
vulkanmod.options.world.fog.tooltip: Haze on the horizon. Its range follows your render distance, so the horizon looks the same at 8 chunks and at 32.
vulkanmod.options.world.clouds.tooltip: How much of the sky the clouds cover. Weather still decides the cloud type.
vulkanmod.options.world.stars.tooltip: How bright the stars burn at night.
vulkanmod.options.world.torch.tooltip: Strength of the light cast by torches, lanterns and lava.
vulkanmod.options.world.emissive.tooltip: How hard glowing texels burn: mob eyes, ore veins, magma.
vulkanmod.options.world.minLight.tooltip: The floor of brightness in total darkness. 0% is pitch black — you will not see your hand.
vulkanmod.options.rt.scale.tooltip: Resolution the rays are traced at, as a share of your screen. Half the width means a quarter of the pixels — and a quarter of the rays. DLSS reconstructs the full frame from it, so 50-67% costs little visible detail. 100% = DLAA (no upscaling, DLSS only denoises).
vulkanmod.options.rt.quality.tooltip: How far the rays fly: shadows, reflections and sky light. A ray reaching 4096 blocks walks the whole BVH tree; one cut to 128 only touches the near branches. Distant shadows drown in fog anyway.
vulkanmod.options.rt.denoiser.tooltip: Which denoiser cleans the traced image. Options marked WIP are not implemented yet and behave as Off.
vulkanmod.options.rt.upscaler.on.tooltip: Trace at a lower resolution and reconstruct to screen size. Off = trace at native resolution (much heavier).
vulkanmod.options.rt.denoiser.on.tooltip: Removes the grain left by ray tracing. Off = raw traced image, noisy — useful for debugging only. Locked to DLSS while the upscaler is DLSS: Ray Reconstruction denoises and upscales in a single pass, so its denoising cannot be declined.
